February 26, 1986 
Walter J. Egan 
505 East Lomita Boulevard, #1 
Carson, CA 
90745 
Re: 
A-86-058 
Dear Mr. Egan: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours,

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Walter J. Egan 
505 East Lomita Blvd., #1 
Carson, 
CA 
90745 
Dear Mr. Egan: 
March 27, 1986 
Re: 
FPPC No. A-86-058 
This is in reply to your letter dated February II, 1986, 
addressed to Robert Leidigh, concerning the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
You enclosed with your letter a copy of a letter and a 
survey which were sent by you to the citizens of Carson. 
You 
indicated that the costs of the mailing were paid for by a 
foundation established by your mother, and asked us to determine 
whether or not the survey is a "non-political activity." 
The Political Reform Act of 1974 provides that all 
"contributions" received by, and all "expenditures" made by a 
candidate or an officeholder must be disclosed on the candidate's 
or officeholder's campaign disclosure statements. 
The Fair Political Practices Commission's regulation 2 Cal. 
Adm. Code section 18215 provides that a "contribution" is a 
payment which is: 
... received by or made at the behest of: 
a candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding 
circumstances that the payment was received or made at 
his or her behest for personal purposes unrelated to 
his or her candidacy or status as an officeholder. 
(Emphasis added) 
FPPC regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18225 provides that 
an "expenditure" is a payment which is: 
... made by a candidate, unless it is clear from 
surrounding circumstances that the payment was made 
for personal purposes unrelated to his or her 
candidacy or status as an officeholder. (Emphasis 
added) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • 
(916) 322-5660 


Walter Egan 
March 27, 1986 
Page 2 
Based on these definitions, it is clear that the costs of the 
letter and survey you sent to the citizens of Carson are 
reportable. 
In addition, it appears from the information provided on the 
"Exemption Application" for the Walter J. Egan Education 
Foundation which you enclosed, that the foundation may meet the 
definition of a "committee" under Gov. Code section 82013(a). 
We 
believe the foundation meets the definition of a committee because 
some of the moneys received by the foundation appear to meet the 
definition of "contributions." 
In addition, at least some of the 
payments made by the foundation a"re " expenditures" (the payments 
for the costs of the letter and survey are "expenditures"). 
If 
the foundation meets the definition of a committee under Gov. Code 
section 82013(a), it must file a statement of Organization (Form 
410) and campaign disclosure statements. 
Please call me at (916) 322-5662, and we can discuss the 
obligation of the-foundation to register and file reports as a 
committee. 
JP:kt 
Sincerely, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief, Technical Assistance 
and Analysis 


Fair political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Attention of Mr. Robert Leidigh: 
Dear Mr. Leidigh: 
505 East Lomita Boulevard 
#1 
Carson, California 90745 
February 11, 1986 
I am writing you in response to a telephone conversation that I had 
with Helen Arriola, Deputy Field Officer for the Los Angeles F.P.P.C. 
office on Monday, February 10, 1986. 
During our conversation 
we discussed a survey that was recently sent 
to the community. 
I am enclosing within this letter a copy of that survey 
as well as a copy of the answer that was sent back to members of the 
community who so requested a response. 
You will also find a copy of a newspaper article that gives the survey 
responses from a former survey in the form of a recap on its front page. 
Some time ago my mother, Rosie T. Egan, wished to establish a foundation 
that would endeavor to cover and report the costs of non-political activities. 
Upon seeking the advice of an attorney, a foundation was formed. 
I am sending you a copy of the first project that the foundation has been 
involved with. 
In discussing the merits of this program, it has been 
suggested to me that I seek the advice of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission on whether or not this would be considered non-political activity. 
I, therefore, have called Helen Arriola and she has indicated to me that 
surveys are normally evaluated on a program by program basis. 
Since I have just finished sending in my annual F.P.P.C. report to 
the City 
Clerk (July 1, 1985 to December 31, 1985), I want to make sure that I have 
done everything to comply with the law. 
Therefore, I am respectfully 
requesting your opinion on whether or not the subject survey is a non-political 
activity. 
Most sincerely, 
Walter

Citizens Express Vievvs 
Councilman Walter J. 
"Jake" Egan, conducted his 
own survey of a substantial 
cross section of Carson 
residents, 
on 
areas of 
citizens concerns, and came 
up with some startling an-
swers. 
As an 
example, 
the 
questionnaire results 
showed that only 25 percent 
of the residents T3ted the 
city's image as "good." 
The majority, 53 percent, 
replied that the profile was 
"fair," and a surprising 22 
percent registered, "poor." 
The survey pointed out 
that the city council has ap-
propriated a substantial 
aBlOunt of money for the 
construction of the new 
Community Building, ap-
proximately $12,900,000. 
The question: "Do you think 
that this new facility should 
be w,et! to house social ser-
vice programs?" 
Two-thirds of the residen-
ts surveyed answered yes, 
68 percent in favor and 32 
against. 
Another question on the 
community building pointed 
out that it is estimated that 
$800,000 a year will be 
nl'eded to maintain the 
building. Egan questioned: 
"Do you think we should 
close parts of the building 
when they are not in use, in 
order to bring the buildings 
maintenance costs down?"

Egan 505 East Lomita Boulevard, #1 Carson, CA 90745 Re: A-86-058 Dear Mr. Egan: Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662. We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 California Fair Political Practices Commission Walter J. Egan 505 East Lomita Blvd., #1 Carson, CA 90745 Dear Mr. Egan: March 27, 1986 Re: FPPC No. A-86-058 This is in reply to your letter dated February II, 1986, addressed to Robert Leidigh, concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. You enclosed with your letter a copy of a letter and a survey which were sent by you to the citizens of Carson. You indicated that the costs of the mailing were paid for by a foundation established by your mother, and asked us to determine whether or not the survey is a "non-political activity." The Political Reform Act of 1974 provides that all "contributions" received by, and all "expenditures" made by a candidate or an officeholder must be disclosed on the candidate's or officeholder's campaign disclosure statements. The Fair Political Practices Commission's regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18215 provides that a "contribution" is a payment which is: ... received by or made at the behest of: a candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding circumstances that the payment was received or made at his or her behest for personal purposes unrelated to his or her candidacy or status as an officeholder. (Emphasis added) FPPC regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18225 provides that an "expenditure" is a payment which is: ... made by a candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding circumstances that the payment was made for personal purposes unrelated to his or her candidacy or status as an officeholder. (Emphasis added) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 Walter Egan March 27, 1986 Page 2 Based on these definitions, it is clear that the costs of the letter and survey you sent to the citizens of Carson are reportable. In addition, it appears from the information provided on the "Exemption Application" for the Walter J. Egan Education Foundation which you enclosed, that the foundation may meet the definition of a "committee" under Gov. Code section 82013(a).
